description: AI is coming for one of America's dirtiest jobs: changing tires. Why it matters: Demand for tire service is accelerating, in part because EV tires need more frequent replacement — just as service shops struggle to hire technicians.Changing tires is noisy, back-breaking work.And nobody wants to spend half their day waiting for a tire rotation. title: Romanian metal, Australian star Delta Goodrem qualify for Eurovision grand final
description: Romanian metal, a Danish club anthem and Australian singer Delta Goodrem advanced to the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 grand final after Thursday’s second semi-final in Vienna. Albania, Bulgaria, Cyprus, the Czech Republic, Malta, Norway and Ukraine also secured places in Saturday’s final at the Wiener Stadthalle.

title: Eurovision braces for new protests over Israel’s participation
description: Eurovision is heading towards its grand final, but organisers are bracing for more demonstrations against Israel.

title: Iran war live: Trump’s visit to China shadowed by conflict with Tehran
description: Irans foreign minister said Iranians never bow to pressure, and there is no military solution to issues with Tehran.

title: Congressional leaders plead with their absentee lawmakers: "You've got to come"
description: Congressional leaders are straining to get their absentee members to return to Capitol Hill as the House's vote margins on major legislation grow ever more precarious.Why it matters: Just on Thursday, an Iran war powers resolution failed to pass in a tie vote. Any lawmaker could have tipped the outcome — and half a dozen were absent."People cannot miss votes," a senior House Democrat told Axios.The truants on that vote included Reps. Tom Kean Jr.

title: China fires verbal warning shot at US over Taiwan
description: Any misstep over Taiwan could push the U.S. and China toward direct confrontation, Chinese leader Xi Jinping told President Donald Trump.

title: UN aid convoy hit by drone strikes in Ukraine’s Kherson
description: A UN humanitarian convoy delivering aid to the city of Kherson was hit twice by drones.

title: Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
description:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia appointed on Saturday a former high court judge as the country’s new anti-graft czar to replace its outgoing chief who was dogged by allegations of abuse of power and illegal shareholding.
	The appointment of Abdul Halim Aman comes as Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s government faces increased scrutiny on promises made to tackle corruption in the Southeast Asian nation.

title: Chinese panda pair headed to US zoo
description:
BEIJING: Two giant pandas from China are headed to Atlanta on a new 10-year conservation deal.
	Pandas Ping Ping, a male, and Fu Shuang, a female, will live at Zoo Atlanta, China Wildlife Conservation Association said in a statement Friday.
	The organization signed the research agreement with the United States zoo last year, it said, noting the deal continues “the ‘panda bond’ shared by the people of both nations for more than 20 years.”

title: Despite Iran tensions, King Charles III will follow his mother’s lead in celebrating US-UK bonds
description:
LONDON: The challenge for King Charles III as he embarks on this week’s state visit to the US is, as always, to live up to his mother’s example.
	The late Queen Elizabeth II wowed Congress in 1991 with a speech that celebrated the shared democratic traditions of Britain and the United States, quoted Abraham Lincoln, Franklin D. Roosevelt and Ralph Waldo Emerson, and highlighted the deep bonds between the two nations.

title: Australia refuses repatriation help for citizens in Syria camp
description:
SYDNEY: Australia refused to help repatriate citizens related to suspected Daesh militants on Saturday, following a report that some Australian families are trying to return home from a camp in Syria.
	National broadcaster ABC said four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren had left the Roj camp in northeastern Syria on Friday, citing the camp director.
	Syrian forces were taking them to Damascus to help them fly back to A

title: Saudi Arabia’s events sector push on track despite disruptions, says industry veteran
description:
RIYADH: Saudi Arabia’s push to position itself as a global events hub remains firmly on track despite geopolitical disruptions, according to one of the leading figures in the industry.

	The US-Israel war with Iran has led to disruptions to many of the Kingdom’s flagship forums, with the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix 2026, the LEAP technology conference, and Automechanika Riyadh among those to be postponed.

title: Startup Wrap: MENA startups draw capital as AI bets continue to accelerate
description:
RIYADH: Startups across the Middle East and North Africa saw new funding rounds over the past week, with acquisitions, and partnerships across artificial intelligence, fintech, gaming, and digital infrastructure.

	Saudi Arabia-based Signit raised $15 million in a series A round led by Raed Ventures, with participation from STV, Seedra Ventures, Takamol Ventures, and Suhail Ventures.

title: US military says it struck vessel in Eastern Pacific, killing two
description:
WASHINGTON: The US military said on Friday it struck a vessel in the Eastern Pacific, killing two people, in the latest such attack, condemned by rights groups as “extrajudicial killings” and described by Washington as targeting “narco-terrorists.”
	The US Southern Command alleged that the vessel struck on Friday was operated by “Designated Terrorist Organizations” that ‌it did ‌not identify.
	It said ‌that ⁠no US military ⁠forces were harmed. It

title: Spain is ‘reliable’ NATO member, PM says after reported US ouster threat
description:
NICOSIA, Cyprus: Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday stressed that his country was a “reliable member” of NATO, after a report the United States was considering trying to suspend it over its refusal to support military operations against Iran.

title: Cuban government says CIA chief John Ratcliffe met with officials in Havana
description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday in a bid to improve dialogue between Washington and the communist-run island, the Cuban government said. The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations,” Havana said. The CIA did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

title: Judge Orders U.S. to Return Colombian Woman Deported to Congo
description: The unusual ruling came after the judge found that the Trump administration had most likely violated the law by deporting the 55-year-old woman to the African country despite its refusal to take her.

title: Cuba runs out of fuel, diesel amid US blockade
description: Cuba has run out of fuel and diesel, as the US blockade plunges the island deeper into an energy crisis. The government is now considering a renewed offer of aid from the Trump administration - but that financing would come with conditions. Also in the show - US President Donald Trump announces China will buy 200 aircraft from Boeing, as he continues his high-stakes visit to Beijing.

title: Nobel winner Mukwege warns of predatory US deal for DR Congo
description: In tonight's edition, Congolese Nobel laureate Denis Mukwege slams the US-brokered peace deal in eastern DRC as predatory. Also, once dominant in Senegal’s economy, France is now being overtaken  as China and Turkey emerge as the country’s leading investors. And we take a squint at the stellar career of South African sensation Tyla.

title: Macron ‘slap’ was sparked by texts with Iranian actress, book claims
description: An AI-generated tabloid magazine cover online claimed to show President Emmanuel Macron and Iranian-French actress Golshifteh Farahani together. The fake cover builds on claims from a new book about the Macrons by a French journalist, who alleges the pair had a “platonic” relationship, of which the text messages prompted the now infamous "slap" by French First Lady Brigitte Macron, as the couple exited the plane in Hanoi.

description: Russia and Ukraine reported fighting along their front line despite agreeing to a ceasefire from May 9 to May 11 as part of a Trump-led push for peace.

title: US Army abruptly cancels deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland
description: The Army has abruptly halted a planned deployment to Poland, bringing U.S. personnel numbers in Europe to pre-2022 levels — before Russia invaded Ukraine.

title: Russia pummels Kyiv, killing at least 10 as advance in the east slows
description: Russia pummelled Kyiv for hours early on Thursday, firing hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in an attack that killed at least 10 people and further shredded hopes of a halt to Moscow's grinding invasion. FRANCE 24's August Hakansson reports.

title: 'A desire to lower the temperature on both sides', expert says as Trump meets Xi
description: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump started a crucial series of meetings in Beijing on Thursday in the U.S.-China summit. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Garret Martin, Lecturer and Co-Director Transatlantic Policy Center at the American University School of International Service, says that "what we saw today was not much in substance but at the very least a desire to lower the temperature on both sides".

title: Iran accuses United Arab Emirates of being 'active partner' in US-Israeli war
description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the United Arab Emirates on Thursday of playing an active role in the US-Israeli war against his country. Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi denied took place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

title: Syria: Prosecuting Assad-era security officials
description: The new Syrian administration is trying to provide justice for the tens of thousands tortured and hundreds of thousands more killed by the regime of former dictator Bashar al-Assad. But is enough being done to investigate and prosecute those accused? Some tentative steps have been taken. A trial is underway in Damascus of Assad himself and his brother Maher, although neither are in the country and are being tried in absentia.

description: Машины используют для разбора завалов

title: PM tells court he’s at ‘top of health scale,’ says now-treated cancer diagnosed late 2025
description:
Trump and Xi discussed Middle East in meeting, says China * IDF issues evacuation warning for 8 villages in south Lebanon * Vessel off UAE coast seized and taken to Iranian waters
The post PM tells court hes at top of health scale, says now-treated cancer diagnosed late 2025 appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: NY school district pays $125K to settle suit over erased pro-Palestinian student art
description:
Long Island district ordered to pay out student whose Palestinian-themed watermelon parking spot art in 2024 was painted over by high school over alleged antisemitic hate speech
The post NY school district pays $125K to settle suit over erased pro-Palestinian student art appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Bone appétit: Paris dogs lap up treats at canine-friendly cafés
description: Dog-friendly cafés and patisseries are popping up across the French capital, home to an estimated 100,000 canines, giving four-legged customers and their owners an opportunity to indulge side-by-side.

title: Russia pounds Kyiv and cities across Ukraine in massive air attack
description: Russia pummeled the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and ​several other regions in a massive aerial attack involving drones and ballistic missiles, killing at least one person and wounding dozens, local officials said Thursday.

title: China's Xi promises more trade but warns Trump over Taiwan as summit kicks off in Beijing
description: China's President Xi Jinping told his US counterpart Donald Trump that Chinese markets will be more open to US and global trade in the future as a two-day summit got under way in Beijing on Thursday but warned that persistent disagreements over Taiwan could send relations down a dangerous path.

title: Nigerian film star Alexx Ekubo dies from cancer aged 40
description: The actor was recognised for his contributions to entertainment as well as his humanitarian efforts.

title: Iran war day 76: Vance says progress made in talks; Israel pounds Lebanon
description: US Vice President Vance says progress is being made in talks with Iran as President Trump begins his trip to China.

title: Trump in China for talks with Xi Jinping
description: Trump visits Beijing for high-stakes summit with Xi as Iran, trade and Taiwan strain US-China ties.

title: Iran’s US-bound World Cup squad given public sendoff in Tehran
description: Thousands of people cheered Team Melli as Irans World Cup kit was unveiled before the teams training camp in Turkiye.

title: The Lecturer and Philosopher King: Xi Jinping Behind Closed Doors
description: Encounters with other world leaders reveal a side of China’s leader that the public rarely sees, and offer clues to how he will approach President Trump in Beijing.

title: Gulf Countries Arrest Shiite ‘Traitors’ Amid War With Iran
description: Dozens of Gulf citizens have been accused of belonging to Iran-linked terrorism cells as the war accelerates a shift toward deeper authoritarianism in the region.

title: Russia-Ukraine War Shows Cease-Fires Have Lost Meaning Under Trump
description: Temporary truces have become a tool of performative diplomacy, an end in themselves rather than a prelude to a lasting settlement, analysts say.

title: Rescuers search rubble of Kyiv flats after massive Russian strikes kill two
description: Children are among the injured after Russia launched drones and missiles across Ukraine, officials say.

title: WSJ: разведка США доложила о большом преимуществе Китая на фоне войны с Ираном
description: Китай получает значительное преимущество перед США, извлекая выгоду из войны на Ближнем Востоке и добиваясь сдвигов в нескольких ключевых областях конкуренции, написано в отчете разведуправления Объединенного штаба Соединенных Штатов. О документе, который представлен перед визитом президента Дональда Трампа в Китай, пишет The Washington Post.
